by
  refine' ⟨_, _⟩
  · -- Prove that f is injective
    have h₁ : Function.Injective (ModuleCat.ofHom (ker_v L).toIntLinearMap) := by
      -- Use the injectivity of the homomorphism (ker_v L).toIntLinearMap
      have h₂ : Function.Injective (ker_v L : 𝒪[L]ˣ → Lˣ) := by
        -- ker_v L is injective because it is a group homomorphism with trivial kernel
        have h₃ : Function.Injective (ker_v L : 𝒪[L]ˣ → Lˣ) := by
          -- This is a direct consequence of the definition of ker_v and the properties of the valuation
          -- Specifically, if ker_v(x) = 1, then x = 1 in 𝒪[L]ˣ
          intro x y h
          simpa [ker_v] using h
        exact h₃
      -- Convert the injectivity to the linear map
      have h₃ : Function.Injective ( (ker_v L : 𝒪[L]ˣ → Lˣ) ) := h₂
      have h₄ : Function.Injective ( (ker_v L).toIntLinearMap : Additive (𝒪[L]ˣ) → Additive (Lˣ) ) := by
        simpa using h₃
      -- Use the injectivity of the linear map to prove the injectivity of the module homomorphism
      exact by
        simpa [ModuleCat.ofHom] using h₄
    -- Use the injectivity to prove that f is injective
    exact by
      simpa [valuationShortComplex, Rep.ofAlgebraAutOnUnits'] using h₁
  · -- Prove that g is surjective
    have h₂ : Function.Surjective (ModuleCat.ofHom (v L).toIntLinearMap) := by
      -- Use the surjectivity of the homomorphism (v L).toIntLinearMap
      have h₃ : Function.Surjective (v L : Lˣ → ℤ) := by
        -- v L is surjective because it is a valuation
        have h₄ : Function.Surjective (v L : Lˣ → ℤ) := by
          -- This is a direct consequence of the properties of the valuation
          -- Specifically, for any integer n, there exists an element in Lˣ with valuation n
          intro n
          -- Use the fact that the valuation is surjective onto the integers
          have h₅ : ∃ (x : Lˣ), v L x = n := by
            -- This is true because the valuation is a group homomorphism onto the integers
            sorry
          obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := h₅
          exact ⟨x, by simp [hx]⟩
        exact h₄
      -- Convert the surjectivity to the linear map
      have h₄ : Function.Surjective ( (v L : Lˣ → ℤ) ) := h₃
      have h₅ : Function.Surjective ( (v L).toIntLinearMap : Additive (Lˣ) → Additive ℤ ) := by
        simpa using h₄
      -- Use the surjectivity of the linear map to prove the surjectivity of the module homomorphism
      exact by
        simpa [ModuleCat.ofHom] using h₅
    -- Use the surjectivity to prove that g is surjective
    exact by
      simpa [valuationShortComplex, Rep.ofAlgebraAutOnUnits'] using h₂

by
  have h₁ : Function.Injective (v L) := (v_injective L).1
  have h₂ : Function.Surjective (v L) := (v_surjective L).1
  have h₃ : Function.Injective (ker_v L).map := (ker_v_injective L).1

  have h₅ : (ker_v L).map.toLinearMap = (ker_v L).map.toLinearMap := rfl
  refine' { _ := _, _ := _ }
  · -- Prove that `f` is injective
    have h₆ : Function.Injective (ker_v L).map := (ker_v_injective L).1
    -- The injectivity of `f` follows from the injectivity of `(ker_v L).map`
    exact by simpa [h₄, h₅] using ModuleHom.injective_iff (Function.Injective.comp h₆ (v_injective _).1)
  · -- Prove that `g` is surjective
    have h₇ : Function.Surjective (v L) := (v_surjective L).1
    -- The surjectivity of `g` follows from the surjectivity of `v L`
    exact by simpa [h₄, h₅] using ModuleHom.surjective_iff (Function.Surjective.comp h₇ (v_surjective _).1)

by
  dsimp only [valuationShortComplex]
  -- Define a temporary structure to access the components of the short complex
  have h₁ : ShortComplex.ShortExact _ := by
    constructor
    · -- Prove that f is injective
      intro x hx
      have h₂ : ModuleCat Maps.Injective _
      exact by
        -- Use the fact that the composition is injective to deduce injectivity of f
        simp_all [ShortComplex, Rep.ofAlgebraAutOnUnits']
        <;>
        (try norm_num) <;>
        (try contradiction) <;>
        (try simp_all [Units.instMulDistribMulAction_right]) <;>
        (try aesop)
    · -- Prove that g is surjective
      intro y
      -- Use the fact that the composition is surjective to deduce surjectivity of g
      simp_all [ShortComplex, Rep.ofAlgebraAutOnUnits']
      <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try { use 0; simp_all }) <;>
      (try { use 1; simp_all }) <;>
      (try aesop)
  exact h₁

by
  refine' ⟨_, _, _⟩
  · -- Prove that `f` is injective
    have h₁ : (valuationShortComplex G K L).f.isInjective := by
      -- Use the fact that the valuation is injective on the units
      have h₂ : (ker_v L : 𝒪[L]ˣ →+* L) = (Additive.of_addHom (Additive.ofUnitHom ring_units_inv (Algebra.toRingHom (by exact specificAlgebra 𝒪[L] L)))) := by
        ext x
        simp [ker_v, specificAlgebra, Additive.of_addHom, Additive.toAddHom]
        <;> aesop
      have h₃ : ModuleCat.ofHom (ker_v L).toIntLinearMap.isInjective := by
        -- Use the fact that the valuation is injective on the units
        have h₄ : (ker_v L : 𝒪[L]ˣ →+* L) = (Additive.of_addHom (Additive.ofUnitHom ring_units_inv (Algebra.toRingHom (by exact specificAlgebra 𝒪[L] L)))) := by
          ext x
          simp [ker_v, specificAlgebra, Additive.of_addHom, Additive.toAddHom]
          <;> aesop
        have h₅ : Function.Injective (Additive.of_addHom (Additive.ofUnitHom ring_units_inv (Algebra.toRingHom (by exact specificAlgebra 𝒪[L] L)) : Additive 𝒪[L]ˣ → Additive (K))).toFun := by
          -- Use the injectivity of the additive map of the specific algebra
          have h₆ : Function.Injective (Additive.ofUnitHom ring_units_inv (Algebra.toRingHom (by exact specificAlgebra 𝒪[L] L)) : 𝒪[L]ˣ → K) := by
            -- Use the injectivity of the specific algebra map
            have h₇ : Function.Injective (Algebra.toRingHom (by exact specificAlgebra 𝒪[K] 𝒪[L]) : 𝒪[K] → Algebra R 𝒪[L]) := by
              apply (algebraMapInjective_iff.mpr _)
              exact by infer_instance
            exact h₇
          exact by
            intro x y hxy
            -- Convert the injectivity to the additive group
            simp_all [Additive.of_addHom, Additive.toAddHom]
            <;> aesop
        -- Convert the injectivity to the linear map
        have h₆ : Function.Injective ((h₄.symm ▸ (Additive.of_addHom (Additive.ofUnitHom ring_units_inv (Algebra.toRingHom (by exact specificAlgebra 𝒪[L] L)) : Additive 𝒪[L]ˣ → Additive (K))).toFun) : Additive 𝒪[L]ˣ → Additive (K)) := by
          intro x y hxy
          apply h₅
          <;> simp_all [Function.funext_iff]
          <;> aesop
        -- Use the injectivity to prove the linear map is injective
        have h₇ : ModuleCat.ofHom (ker_v L).toIntLinearMap.isInjective := by
          -- Use the fact that the additive map is injective
          have h₈ : Function.Injective ((h₄.symm ▸ (Additive.of_addHom (Additive.ofUnitHom ring_units_inv (Algebra.toRingHom (by exact specificAlgebra 𝒪[L] L)) : Additive 𝒪[L]ˣ → Additive (K))).toFun) : Additive 𝒪[L]ˣ → Additive (K)) := by
            intro x y hxy
            apply h₅
            <;> simp_all [Function.funext_iff]
            <;> aesop
          -- Use the injectivity to prove the linear map is injective
          simp_all [ModuleCat.ofHom, LinearMap.toFun, Int.cast_inj]
          <;> aesop
        exact h₇
      -- Use the injectivity of the linear map to prove `f` is injective
      have h₄ : (valuationShortComplex G K L).f.isInjective := by
        simp_all [ModuleCat.ofHom, LinearMap.toFun, Int.cast_inj]
        <;> aesop
      exact h₄
    exact h₁
  · -- Prove that `g` is surjective
    have h₂ : (valuationShortComplex G K L).g.isSurjective := by
      -- Use the fact that the valuation is surjective onto the integers
      have h₃ : Function.Surjective (v L : Lˣ → ℤ) := by
        -- Use the surjectivity of the valuation in a non-archimedean local field
        have h₄ : Function.Surjective (v L : Lˣ → ℤ) := by
          -- Extract the relevant property from the non-archimedean local field instance
          exact IsNonarchimedeanLocalField.surj_v (by infer_instance : IsNonarchimedeanLocalField L)
        exact h₄
      have h₄ : ModuleCat.ofHom (v L).toIntLinearMap.isSurjective := by
        -- Convert the surjectivity of the valuation to the linear map
        have h₅ : Function.Surjective (v L : Lˣ → ℤ) := h₃
        have h₆ : Function.Surjective ((v L : Lˣ → ℤ) : Lˣ → ℤ) := h₅
        -- Use the surjectivity to prove the linear map is surjective
        have h₇ : ModuleCat.ofHom (v L).toIntLinearMap.isSurjective := by
          -- Use the fact that the linear map is surjective
          have h₈ : Function.Surjective ((v L : Lˣ → ℤ) : Lˣ → ℤ) := h₆
          have h₉ : ModuleCat.ofHom (v L).toIntLinearMap.isSurjective := by
            -- Use the surjectivity of the valuation to prove the linear map is surjective
            simp_all [ModuleCat.ofHom, LinearMap.toFun, Int.cast_inj]
            <;>
            (try simp_all [Function.Surjective]) <;>
            (try exact by
              rintro _ ⟨x, hx⟩
              refine' ⟨x, _⟩
              <;> simp_all [LinearMap.toFun, Int.cast_inj]
              <;> aesop) <;>
            (try
              {
                aesop
              })
            <;>
            (try
              {
                simp_all [Function.Surjective]
                <;>
                (try
                  {
                    rintro _ ⟨x, hx⟩
                    refine' ⟨x, _⟩
                    <;> simp_all [LinearMap.toFun, Int.cast_inj]
                    <;> aesop
                  })
              })
          exact h₉
        exact h₇
      -- Use the surjectivity of the linear map to prove `g` is surjective
      have h₅ : (valuationShortComplex G K L).g.isSurjective := by
        simp_all [ModuleCat.ofHom, LinearMap.toFun, Int.cast_inj]
        <;> aesop
      exact h₅
    exact h₂
  · -- Prove that `f` maps to the kernel of `g`
    have h₃ : (valuationShortComplex G K L).f.ker = (valuationShortComplex G K L).g.ker := by
      -- Use the fact that the image of `f` is the kernel of `g`
      have h₄ : (valuationShortComplex G K L).f.map = (valuationShortComplex G K L).g.ker := by
        -- Use the definition of the kernel and image
        rw [show (valuationShortComplex G K L).g.ker = LinearMap.ker (v L).toIntLinearMap by rfl]
        rw [show (valuationShortComplex G K L).f.map = LinearMap.range (ker_v L).toIntLinearMap by rfl]
        -- Prove that the image of `f` is the kernel of `g`
        have h₅ : LinearMap.range (ker_v L).toIntLinearMap = LinearMap.ker (v L).toIntLinearMap := by
          -- Use the fact that the valuation is 0 on the kernel
          have h₆ : (ker_v L : 𝒪[L]ˣ →+* L) = (Additive.of_addHom (Additive.ofUnitHom ring_units_inv (Algebra.toRingHom (by exact specificAlgebra 𝒪[L] L)))) := by
            ext x
            simp [ker_v, specificAlgebra, Additive.of_addHom, Additive.toAddHom]
            <;> aesop
          have h₇ : LinearMap.range (ker_v L).toIntLinearMap = LinearMap.ker (v L).toIntLinearMap := by
            -- Use the fact that the valuation is 0 on the kernel
            simp_all [ModuleCat.ofHom, LinearMap.toFun, Int.cast_inj]
            <;>
            (try simp_all [Function.Injective, Function.Surjective]) <;>
            (try
              {
                apply LinearMap.ext
                intro x
                simp_all [LinearMap.mem_ker, LinearMap.mem_range]
                <;>
                (try
                  {
                    aesop
                  })
                <;>
                (try
                  {
                    simp_all [Function.Injective, Function.Surjective]
                    <;>
                    aesop
                  })
              })
            <;>
            (try
              {
                aesop
              })
          exact h₇
        exact h₅
      -- Use the fact that the image of `f` is the kernel of `g`
      have h₅ : (valuationShortComplex G K L).f.ker = (valuationShortComplex G K L).g.ker := by
        -- Use the definition of the kernel and image
        simp_all [ShortComplex.ker, LinearMap.ker_eq_bot]
        <;>
        (try aesop)
        <;>
        (try simp_all [LinearMap.ker_eq_bot])
        <;>
        (try aesop)
      exact h₅
    -- Use the fact that the image of `f` is the kernel of `g`
    exact by
      simp_all [ShortComplex.ker, LinearMap.ker_eq_bot]
      <;>
      (try aesop)
      <;>
      (try simp_all [LinearMap.ker_eq_bot])
      <;>
      (try aesop)

  have h₁ : (valuationShortComplex G K L).f.monMap := by
    apply ModuleCat.ofHom_injective

    <;> exact (v L).toLinearMap.ker_eq_bot_of_surjective (v_surjective L) |>.symm
  have h₃ : (valuationShortComplex G K L).f.range = ⊥ := by
    apply eq_bot_iff.mpr
    intro y hy
    obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := hy
    have h₄ : (ker_v L).toIntLinearMap x = 0 := by simpa using x.2
    have h₅ : (ker_v L : Submodule ℤ (Additive (Kˣ))) x = 0 := by simpa using h₄
    simpa [Submodule.mem_bot] using h₅
  have h₄ : (valuationShortComplex G K L).g.ker = ⊤ := by
    apply eq_top_iff.mpr
    intro x _
    have h₅ : x ∈ (v L).toLinearMap.ker := by
      simp [LinearMap.mem_ker]
      <;>
      (try aesop)
      <;>
      (try simp_all [v_surjective])
      <;>
      (try
        {
          obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := (v_surjective L).surjective x
          simp_all [v_stalk]
          <;>
          aesop
        })
    have h₆ : x = 0 := by
      have h₇ : (v L).toLinearMap.ker = ⊥ := by
        exact (v L).toLinearMap.ker_eq_bot_of_surjective (v_surjective L)
      simp_all [Submodule.mem_bot]
    simp_all
  exact ⟨h₁, h₂, h₃, h₄⟩

by
  have h₁ : Function.Injective (ModuleCat.ofHom (ker_v L).toIntLinearMap : Rep.ofAlgebraAutOnUnits' G 𝒪[K] 𝒪[L] → Rep.ofAlgebraAutOnUnits' G K L) := by
    -- Prove that the kernel of the valuation map is injective
    -- This follows from the fact that the valuation map is injective on the units
    sorry
  have h₂ : Function.Surjective (ModuleCat.ofHom (v L).toIntLinearMap : Rep.ofAlgebraAutOnUnits' G K L →+ ℤ[G]) := by
    -- Prove that the valuation map is surjective
    -- This follows because the valuation map is surjective onto ℤ
    sorry
  -- Combine the above results to show the sequence is exact
  sorry
